points.text: Erasca, Inc. faces a securities class action following a $2.8 billion market cap loss after Revolution Medicines accused the company of patent infringement regarding its pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015 and a patient death occurred one month post-treatment.

Revolution Medicines accused Erasca of patent infringement and trade secret misappropriation concerning the pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015, challenging Erasca's intellectual property claims and best-in-class narrative.

Erasca, Inc. — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors — Pan-RAS molecular glue development halted by securities class action; patient death reported one month post-dose; Revolution Medicines alleges trade secret misappropriation and improper comparison to RMC-6236.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Pan-RAS molecular glue candidate; patient death reported one month post-dosing; patent infringement lawsuit filed by Revolution Medicines challenging IP validity and comparing 40mg cohort to RMC-6236 400mg cohort.

Erasca, Inc. — Pan-RAS molecular glue commercialization — ERAS-0015 — Stock dropped 48% ($9.25) on April 27, 2026 disclosures regarding IP disputes with Revolution Medicines and a fatal adverse event; in-licensed patent family from Joyo under legal challenge.

Revolution Medicines — Intellectual property enforcement — RMC-6236 (competitor to ERAS-0015) — Accused Erasca of misappropriating trade secrets and making improper dose comparisons between RMC-6236 and ERAS-0015.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational (previously compared to 40mg cohort vs RevMed's 400mg RMC-6236) — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma — Outcome: Patient death reported one month after treatment; trial status suspended pending legal resolution.

Erasca, Inc. — Securities class action litigation — ERAS-0015 IP dispute — Hagens Berman Sobol Shapiro LLP filed suit on behalf of investors (Class Period: Jan 14, 2025–Apr 26, 2026) alleging misleading statements on ERAS-0015 equivalence to RMC-6236 and IP validity; market cap loss of $2.8 billion.
